description: The following recommendations should be considered while configuring the IP addresses for primary and backup controllers: While configuring a primary-controller ip <ipv4-address>, it is recommended to use the IP address of a standalone controller or an IP address of a controller in the primary cluster. Similarly, while configuring a backup-controller ip <ipv4-address>, it is recommended to use the IP address of a standalone controller or an IP address of a controller in the backup cluster. Configuring both primary or backup controller IP addresses from the same cluster is not supported.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for User Based Tunnel?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - The following recommendations should be considered while configuring the IP addresses for primary and backup controllers: While configuring a primary-controller ip <ipv4-address>, it is recommended to use the IP address of a standalone controller or an IP address of a controller in the primary cluster. Similarly, while configuring a backup-controller ip <ipv4-address>, it is recommended to use the IP address of a standalone controller or an IP address of a controller in the backup cluster. Configuring both primary or backup controller IP addresses from the same cluster is not supported.

description: User-Based Tunneling (UBT) and DHCPv4/v6 snooping are mutually exclusive features in AOS-CX. When these two features are configured simultaneously, binding entries for the end clients will not be populated in the binding database. IP source-lockdown feature permits traffic from the clients to pass through the switch only if the client's MAC and IP addresses are present in the binding database. IP source lockdown can be enabled at both port and VLAN levels. The port-level configuration will be overridden by the VLAN-level configuration only when the port is a member of a single VLAN. If the port is a member of multiple VLANs, then the VLAN-level configuration for IP source lockdown will not be applied. Client IP tracker can track the IP address until the IP source lockdown is applied. Once the lockdown is in effect, the IP address will only be tracked if there is a valid entry in the binding table. However, if an IP address is already being tracked, it will remain active through probes, as probes utilize ARP packets, which are Layer 2 (L2) packets rather than Layer 3 (L3) packets.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for Client IP Tracker?

Feature Caveat: Client IP Tracker - User-Based Tunneling (UBT) and DHCPv4/v6 snooping are mutually exclusive features in AOS-CX. When these two features are configured simultaneously, binding entries for the end clients will not be populated in the binding database. IP source-lockdown feature permits traffic from the clients to pass through the switch only if the client's MAC and IP addresses are present in the binding database. IP source lockdown can be enabled at both port and VLAN levels. The port-level configuration will be overridden by the VLAN-level configuration only when the port is a member of a single VLAN. If the port is a member of multiple VLANs, then the VLAN-level configuration for IP source lockdown will not be applied. Client IP tracker can track the IP address until the IP source lockdown is applied. Once the lockdown is in effect, the IP address will only be tracked if there is a valid entry in the binding table. However, if an IP address is already being tracked, it will remain active through probes, as probes utilize ARP packets, which are Layer 2 (L2) packets rather than Layer 3 (L3) packets.

description: NOTE In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for ACLs?

Feature Caveat: ACLs - NOTE In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

description: VSF stack formation is blocked when there are reserved autojoin interfaces (25, 26, 49, 50) in the stack topology.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for Aruba CX Mobile App?
Feature Caveat: Aruba CX Mobile App - VSF stack formation is blocked when there are reserved autojoin interfaces (25, 26, 49, 50) in the stack topology.

description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?

Feature Caveat: Classifiers - For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for CMF?
Feature Caveat: CMF - No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: The switch may only software forward at a rate of 100pps if the packets that trigger ICMP redirect.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for ICMP Redirect?
Feature Caveat: ICMP Redirect - The switch may only software forward at a rate of 100pps if the packets that trigger ICMP redirect.

description: Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

description: Ensure the cipher suite GCM-AES-128 is configured when AOS-CX is acting as a key server. This is because, by default AOS-CX will use the most secure cipher suite gcm-aes-xpn-256 for establishing MACsec secure link as Comware/PVOS doesn’t support an XPN cipher suite.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - Ensure the cipher suite GCM-AES-128 is configured when AOS-CX is acting as a key server. This is because, by default AOS-CX will use the most secure cipher suite gcm-aes-xpn-256 for establishing MACsec secure link as Comware/PVOS doesn’t support an XPN cipher suite.

description: When Cisco AnyConnect is used as dot1x supplicant, it is recommended to configure cak-length to be 16 under dot1x-authenticator mode.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what limitation is mentioned for MACsec?
Feature Caveat: MACsec - When Cisco AnyConnect is used as dot1x supplicant, it is recommended to configure cak-length to be 16 under dot1x-authenticator mode.

description: MACsec works between a CX device and a Windows VM running AnyConnect with AES-128 cipher. AnyConnect does not support AES-256 in the NAM module (works only for the VPN module).

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - MACsec works between a CX device and a Windows VM running AnyConnect with AES-128 cipher. AnyConnect does not support AES-256 in the NAM module (works only for the VPN module).

description: OSPFv2 and OSPFv3 do not support detailed LSA show commands.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for OSPF?
Feature Caveat: OSPF - OSPFv2 and OSPFv3 do not support detailed LSA show commands.

description: Authorization by means of HPE VSAs is not supported.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for RADIUS?
Feature Caveat: RADIUS - Authorization by means of HPE VSAs is not supported.

description: REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for REST?
Feature Caveat: REST - REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: RIP/RIPng metric configuration support is not available.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for RIP/RIPng?
Feature Caveat: RIP/RIPng - RIP/RIPng metric configuration support is not available.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: The switch does not support double encapsulation. A packet can be encapsulated with either L2GRE (UBT) or VXLAN, but not both. The network admin should decide the tunneling type to be used, and then plan the configuration accordingly.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what limitation is mentioned for User Based Tunnel?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - The switch does not support double encapsulation. A packet can be encapsulated with either L2GRE (UBT) or VXLAN, but not both. The network admin should decide the tunneling type to be used, and then plan the configuration accordingly.

description: In the event of license issues when onboarding a DUT to primary or backup mobility conductor, the DUT will not try to bootstrap to another mobility conductor where a license is available. For example, if a mobility conductor does not have a license to onboard the DUT but the backup mobility conductor does have adequate licenses, if both mobility conductors are reachable then UBT will be down, and the DUT will not attempt to bootstrap to the backup controller. However, if the primary mobility conductor is not reachable, the DUT gets tunneled to the standby/backup mobility conductor. Once the primary mobility conductor is reachable by the DUT once again, the DUT will not automatically bootstrap back to the primary. Network administrators should manually disable and enable UBT on the DUT to re-establish the tunnel to the primary mobility conductor.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what additional caveat is documented for User Based Tunnel?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - In the event of license issues when onboarding a DUT to primary or backup mobility conductor, the DUT will not try to bootstrap to another mobility conductor where a license is available. For example, if a mobility conductor does not have a license to onboard the DUT but the backup mobility conductor does have adequate licenses, if both mobility conductors are reachable then UBT will be down, and the DUT will not attempt to bootstrap to the backup controller. However, if the primary mobility conductor is not reachable, the DUT gets tunneled to the standby/backup mobility conductor. Once the primary mobility conductor is reachable by the DUT once again, the DUT will not automatically bootstrap back to the primary. Network administrators should manually disable and enable UBT on the DUT to re-establish the tunnel to the primary mobility conductor.

description: The R8V13A 6200F Switch does not support mixed VSF stacking.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for VSF?
Feature Caveat: VSF - The R8V13A 6200F Switch does not support mixed VSF stacking.

description: Only static VXLAN is supported.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?
Feature Caveat: VXLAN - Only static VXLAN is supported.

description: UBT VLAN local mode does not support multiple MAC addresses or clients in same VLAN on the same switch port, as this configuration can cause mac-move issues on an L2 switch connected to a UBT Switch. Use UBT vlan-extend mode to support multiple MAC address or clients in a differently tagged VLAN on a single switchport.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for UBT Design?

Feature Caveat: UBT Design - UBT VLAN local mode does not support multiple MAC addresses or clients in same VLAN on the same switch port, as this configuration can cause mac-move issues on an L2 switch connected to a UBT Switch. Use UBT vlan-extend mode to support multiple MAC address or clients in a differently tagged VLAN on a single switchport.

description: Symptom: The webserver becomes unresponsive, but AFC is reachable. Restarting HTTPS and Nginx services does not recover connectivity. Netstat for ports 443 and 80 do not work as expected. Scenario: In some scenarios where there is an excessive memory usage and resource exhaustion, the nginx process is incorrectly terminated by the switch leading to webservice to become unresponsive.

description: Symptom: Unexpected multicast flooding on VLAN after upgrading from 10.10 to 10.13. Scenario: In some scenarios when upgrading from 10.10 to 10.13, IGMP flooding can be observed for VLANs that have IP IGMP snooping enabled. Workaround: Disable and re-enable IP IGMP snooping on affected VLAN.

description: Symptom: Packets near MTU limits in size may not be present in a port or tunnel/remote based mirror. Scenario: When a client configures a mirror where the destination interface has the same MTU size, mirrored packets that are close to max MTU are incorrectly dropped and not mirrored. This applies to remote/tunnel mirrors as well. Workaround: Configuring mirror destination ports as routing port and set the IP MTU size. For a VLAN interface, set the IP MTU on the VLAN SVI. IP MTU must be set to accommodate the ethernet overhead +tag. For the default MTU, 1500, the IP MTU would be 1518. For a tunneled scenario, the MTU must also account for the tunnel header.

description: Symptom: Delay of ~30s observed when updating VLAN access config via snmpset and verifying via snmpget immediately after. Scenario: When snmpset changes one OID and snmpget reads another OID before the cache can result in not updated cache values for OID. Workaround: Use the CLI for VLAN access configuration instead of SNMP.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.
